pattern: Breadth-first search over buses rather than stops: from the buses serving the current reachable stops, hop to every connected bus, counting transfers as BFS layers.
hints: Boarding a bus lets you reach every stop on its route, so the natural unit of distance is the number of buses ridden, not stops travelled.
Index which buses pass through each stop, then treat two buses as adjacent when they share any stop.
BFS from all buses serving the source stop; the first layer whose route includes the target gives the minimum bus count, and exhausting the queue without reaching it means it is impossible.

edge_inputs.description: two buses connected by a shared stop
disconnected routes make the target unreachable
source already equals target needs zero buses
single bus directly connecting source and target
target stop exists but on no route reachable from source
source not served by any bus is impossible unless it is the target

stress.description: random routes over a modest stop universe so chains of transfers and dead ends both occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num_routes = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 60)))
    max_stop = 40
    routes = []
    for _ in range(num_routes):
        length = rng.randint(1, 5)
        stops = rng.sample(range(max_stop), length)
        routes.append(stops)
    source = rng.randint(0, max_stop - 1)
    target = rng.randint(0, max_stop - 1)
    return [routes, source, target]
stress.note: small stop universe forces overlapping routes and reachable/unreachable mixes

complexity.time: O(sum route lengths squared worst case)
